statement: The standard genetic code is significantly error-minimizing under four independent physicochemical distance metrics: Grantham (p=0.003), Miyata (p=0.001), Woese polar requirement (p=0.004), and Kyte-Doolittle hydropathy (p=0.001)
26 of 27 NCBI translation tables remain in the top 5% of their own block-preserving null for Grantham edge-mismatch (BH-FDR corrected); only translation table 3 (yeast mito) exceeds the threshold
Coloring optimality is robust across all diagonal-edge weights rho in [0,1], including the full Hamming graph H(3,4) = K_4 □ K_4 □ K_4 of single-nucleotide substitutions (p<0.01 at all rho values)

Natural codon reassignments are depleted for topology-breaking changes — moves that fragment an amino acid's codon family — at approximately 21% of observed events vs 66-73% of the candidate landscape, robust to adjacency definition (Q_6 vs the full Hamming graph H(3,4)) and clade exclusion

Organisms with variant genetic codes show elevated tRNA gene copy numbers for the reassigned amino acid, verified by tRNAscan-SE 2.0.12 on 18 NCBI genome assemblies across 5 variant genetic codes (24 pairings, 6 independent)
Codon reassignment bit-flip distribution shows positional skew in GF(2)^6 coordinates, but signal is marginal after correcting for non-independence (de-duplicated p=0.075)

tRNA gene duplication accompanies codon reassignment in large nuclear genomes (ciliates, yeasts) but not in streamlined genomes (Blastocrithidia: anticodon stem shortening; Mycoplasma: anticodon modification)

Serine's extreme Atchley Factor 3 score (F3 = −4.760, most extreme of 20 amino acids, 2.24 SD below mean) converges with the GF(2)^6 topological disconnection: F3 captures the mismatch between Serine's small physicochemical footprint and its disproportionate codon diversity, which the geometric framework identifies as maximal inter-family Hamming distance among 6-codon amino acids (4 vs 1 for Leu and Arg)

Systematic survey of Hamming-graph connectivity across 27 NCBI translation tables identifies 4 lineage-collapsed variant-code amino-acid disconnections at epsilon=1 under the default encoding: Thr in yeast mitochondrial code (table 3), Leu in chlorophycean mitochondrial codes (tables 16 and 22, collapsed to a single algal-mito event), Ala in Pachysolen tannophilus nuclear code (table 26), and tripartite Ser in Candida-clade alternative yeast nuclear code (table 12)
XOR 'Fano' relationships in GF(2)^6 predict enrichment of specific amino acids at KRAS G12 co-mutation sites
Serine's minimum UCN-AGY Hamming distance = 4 holds invariantly across all 24 base-to-bit encodings
PSL(2,7) is the fundamental symmetry group of the genetic code
The coordinate-wise map GF(2)^6 -> C^3 sending base-pairs to fourth roots of unity is a holomorphic embedding extending a character of GF(8)*
All 2-fold synonymous codon pairs in the standard code differ at exactly bit position 5 under the default encoding
All 4-fold synonymous codon groups share a 4-bit prefix with 2-bit suffixes exhausting GF(2)^2
